headline: The Cypherpunk Library Preserves Essential Privacy Manifestos
lede: A new digital collection aggregates foundational texts on cryptography and decentralization. It serves as an archive for the intellectual roots of the privacy movement, though the interface currently struggles to match the weight of its contents.

headline: Dopamine Fracking Commodities Human Attention for Profit
lede: Modern digital ecosystems increasingly optimize for addictive feedback loops rather than human depth. We examine the systemic erosion of nuanced experience in an era of engineered cultural consumption.
pullquote: We are harvesting the nuance of human experience to feed an endless machine of synthetic feedback loops

headline: Microsoft Enforces Deletion Policy for Unlicensed Cloud Accounts
lede: Data stored in inactive, unlicensed OneDrive accounts will face permanent deletion beginning July 2026. Organizations must audit their storage configurations immediately to prevent the irreversible loss of critical institutional documentation.

headline: Corporate Breach Disclosure Lag Increases Despite New Regulations
lede: Companies are increasingly prioritizing litigation defense over victim notification when data security fails. Forensic complexity is frequently cited as a shield, while the real-world wait times for critical alerts continue to climb.

headline: The Evolving Role of the AI-Native Software Engineer
lede: As agentic tooling automates routine coding, developers are questioning the long-term viability of current career paths. We look at the shift from manual authorship to the management of automated reasoning systems.

headline: A Family Reclaims the Rituals of Home Burial
lede: Four siblings navigated the legal and physical challenges of a green burial on their own property. By crafting their own casket and managing the labor themselves, they transformed a standard administrative process into an act of intimate remembrance.

headline: Teenage Engineering Unveils Professional Analog Record Cutter
lede: The APC-2 brings precision direct-drive disk cutting to the independent studio environment. Weighing 140 kilograms, this steel and granite unit aims to bridge the gap between high-fidelity mastering and personalized analog production.

headline: Overcoming Systemic Barriers Through Self-Taught Engineering
lede: Persistence in the face of incarceration and addiction provides a lens into the rigid gatekeeping of the modern tech industry. Evidence suggests that prioritizing skill acquisition over traditional credentials offers a pathway for professional reintegration.

headline: Building a Perceptron from Scratch in Python
lede: Understanding the core mechanics of machine learning requires a firm grasp on weights, biases, and training epochs. This guide simplifies the math behind the neural perceptron to explain how machines learn to define boundaries.

headline: Richard Scolyer Leaves a Legacy of Experimental Medicine
lede: Professor Richard Scolyer’s participation in world-first immunotherapy trials for glioblastoma has opened new doors for brain cancer research. His life and work underscore a courageous, data-driven response to one of medicine's most difficult diagnoses.
